lastReleaseMessage: preset recall filters dangling custom-section keys; spec sec5+sec8 documented

barRaise.topFinding: Fifteenth consecutive daily review, zero application-code findings, and today's 06-22 canonical audit again reads fully aligned at v0.18.4 across every counted asset (199 herbs, 75 illustrations, 11 symbols, 16 textures, 24 rune meanings). The code is not the problem and has not been for two weeks. HEAD is still 3d9b480, now eight days frozen (06-14 to 06-22). The 863.98 MiB .git, the stale .git/index.lock, and the uncommitted PROJECT_SPEC.md edit are byte-for-byte the same artifacts the last eight reports listed. The one thing that moved is the wrong direction: the shared YaE work queue grew from 65 to 69 items and a new auto-generated apothecary item, wts-2026-06-22-yaa-stale-project-spec, landed with neither a prompt nor a title, so a watcher is now manufacturing payload-less tickets off a state this review has flagged closeable nine times. The central action (cut v1.0, mark 'Symbol + label polish' done, drop Apothecary to weekly review) is on its tenth consecutive review unactioned. Stalled is the honest read; the verdict is held there to stop the daily reprint loop and force the decision, not because the code regressed.

actions.title: Cut v1.0, mark "Symbol + label polish" done, and drop Apothecary to weekly (constellation-only) review. Tenth review carrying this; third flagged HIGH.
Decide and close the 864 MB `.git` bloat: `git filter-repo --strip-blobs-bigger-than 5M` + force-push, or a "Won't fix" locked decision in PROJECT_SPEC section 8.

Normalize the apothecary queue to one project ID (`yaa`) with a `prompt` field, close the shipped `yaa-commit-release-automsg-2026-06-12`, fix or drop the payload-less `wts-2026-06-22-yaa-stale-project-spec`, and clear the YaE-side mirror-stale-tree item. (Orchestration side; read-only here.)
